900	\begin{lemma}
901	
902	[Coface match: the section {\v C}ech differential is the localisation differential]
903	  \label{lem:section_cech_coface_match}
904	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechCofaceMatch,
905	    AlgebraicGeometry.phiL_naturality,
906	    AlgebraicGeometry.phi_naturality}
907	  \uses{lem:section_cech_objd_apply, def:qcoh_sections_localized, def:section_cech_module_complex}
908	  Take \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\), the standard affine cover \(\mathcal{U}\)
909	  associated with a spanning family \(s : \iota \to R\), and \(\mathcal{F} =
910	  \widetilde{M}\) the quasi-coherent sheaf associated with an \(R\)-module \(M\)
911	  (for a general quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\), see the note following the statement). In
912	  the situation of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}, the degree-\(p\)
913	  differential of the section {\v C}ech complex
914	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\), read through the product
915	  equivalence, is exactly the localisation differential \(\operatorname{dDiff}\) of the
916	  module complex \(D^\bullet\) of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex}.
917	  Concretely, under the per-coordinate identifications each section group
918	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is the away localisation \(M_{s_\sigma}\) and each face
919	  restriction \(\operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)\) is the away-localisation
920	  coface \(\operatorname{dCoface}\), so the alternating sum of face restrictions of
921	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply} becomes the alternating sum of localisation
922	  cofaces, i.e.\ \(\operatorname{dDiff}\).
923	\end{lemma}
924	
925	% NOTE: For the tilde case \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) the per-coordinate
926	% comparison isomorphism and the naturality square follow directly from
927	% \(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\). For a general quasi-coherent
928	% \(\mathcal{F}\), reduce to the tilde case via \(\mathcal{F} \cong
929	% \widetilde{\Gamma\mathcal{F}}\) (Stacks Tag 01I8), the affine equivalence
930	% \(\operatorname{QCoh}(\operatorname{Spec} R) \simeq \operatorname{Mod}(R)\),
931	% which is the open gap recorded in Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}.
932	\begin{proof}
933	  \uses{lem:section_cech_objd_apply, def:section_cech_module_complex, def:qcoh_sections_localized}
934	  The proof layers in two steps.
935	
936	  \emph{(i) Abstract unfold.} By Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply}, the
937	  \(\sigma\)-coordinate of the degree-\(p\) differential, read through the product
938	  equivalence, is the alternating sum
939	  \(\sum_{i} (-1)^i\, \operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)\) of presheaf face
940	  restrictions applied to the deleted-face coordinates. This fixes the shape of the
941	  differential without any sheaf identification of the restrictions.
942	
943	  \emph{(ii) Tilde \(\mathcal{F}\)-bridge.} For \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) each
944	  face restriction is identified with the localisation coface
945	  \(\operatorname{SectionCechModule.dCoface}\) by transporting along a per-coordinate
946	  isomorphism. The section group
947	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) and the localised module
948	  \(M_{s_\sigma} = \operatorname{LocalizedModule}(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers}
949	  s_\sigma)\, M\) both localise \(M\) at the submonoid of powers of
950	  \(s_\sigma = \prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\): the former by
951	  \(\operatorname{qcohSectionsAwayLocalized}\) together with the multi-index
952	  intersection identity \(\operatorname{basicOpen\_sprod}\) (which rewrites
953	  \(\bigcap_k D(s_{\sigma(k)})\) as the single basic open \(D(s_\sigma)\)), the latter
954	  by construction. Hence there is a canonical \(R\)-linear isomorphism
955	  \[
956	    \varphi_\sigma :
957	      \operatorname{ToType}\bigl(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\bigr)
958	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
959	      M_{s_\sigma},
960	  \]
961	  the comparison of the two \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) structures, unique by
962	  \(\operatorname{AwayComparison.comparison\_unique}\) and supplied by
963	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule.iso}\). The naturality square
964	  \[
965	    \varphi_\sigma \;\circ\; \operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)
966	      \;=\;
967	      \operatorname{dCoface} \;\circ\; \varphi_{\sigma \circ d_i}
968	  \]
969	  is exactly \(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\) of
970	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: the presheaf restriction of
971	  \(\widetilde{M}\) between the two section groups is the away-localisation comparison
972	  of the two localisation units. This face-naturality is recorded in two forms: at the
973	  bare localisation level as \(\operatorname{phiL\_naturality}\) and at the sheaf level
974	  (with \(\varphi_\sigma = \operatorname{phi}\)) as \(\operatorname{phi\_naturality}\).
975	  Substituting (ii) into (i) and summing with signs
976	  identifies the section {\v C}ech differential with \(\operatorname{dDiff}\).
977	
978	  The isomorphism \(\varphi_\sigma\) is obtained by matching the two descriptions of
979	  the sections of \(\widetilde{M}\) over \(D(s_\sigma)\): the cosimplicial functor
980	  provides one description and the localisation property of
981	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} provides the other. Their agreement,
982	  together with the naturality square, is the content of this bridge.
983	\end{proof}

985	\begin{lemma}
986	[Abelian-group exactness criterion for the section {\v C}ech complex]
987	  \label{lem:section_cech_ab_exact}
988	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechAbExact,
989	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact,
990	    AlgebraicGeometry.ab_hom_finsetSum_apply,
991	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionToModuleAddEquiv,
992	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionToModuleAddEquiv_apply,
993	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionProdEquiv_symm_apply}
994	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, lem:section_cech_objd_apply,
995	    lem:section_cech_product_equiv, lem:section_cech_coface_match,
996	    lem:section_cech_module_exact}
997	  For \(1 \leq p\) the homology of the section {\v C}ech complex
998	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
999	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} vanishes,
1000	  \(\operatorname{IsZero}(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
1001	  \mathcal{F}).\mathrm{homology}\, p)\). The argument splits into a homological
1002	  precursor and an unconditional ladder transport.
1003	  \begin{itemize}
1004	    \item The precursor \(\operatorname{sectionCech\_isZero\_homology\_of\_objD\_exact}\)
1005	      is pure homological algebra: \emph{given}
1006	      \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\) of the underlying group homomorphisms of two
1007	      consecutive coface differentials \(\operatorname{objD} q\),
1008	      \(\operatorname{objD}(q+1)\), the homology of the section complex at degree
1009	      \(q+1\) is zero. It chains
1010	      \(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\),
1011	      \(\operatorname{HomologicalComplex.exactAt\_iff'}\) (to expose the short complex
1012	      around the node), and the abelian-group exactness criterion
1013	      \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.ab\_exact\_iff\_function\_exact}\) (which states that
1014	      a short complex of abelian groups is exact iff for every \(x_2\) with
1015	      \(g(x_2) = 0\) there is \(x_1\) with \(f(x_1) = x_2\)).
1016	    \item The unconditional \(\operatorname{sectionCechAbExact}\) discharges that
1017	      hypothesis by transporting the already-proved \(R\)-module exactness
1018	      \(\operatorname{dDiff\_exact}\) of
1019	      Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact} across the degreewise additive
1020	      isomorphism, using the ladder-transport lemma
1021	      \(\operatorname{Function.Exact.of\_ladder\_addEquiv\_of\_exact}\).
1022	  \end{itemize}
1023	\end{lemma}
1024	
1025	\begin{proof}
1026	  \uses{lem:section_cech_objd_apply, lem:section_cech_product_equiv,
1027	    lem:section_cech_coface_match, lem:section_cech_module_exact}
1028	  Fix \(p = q + 1 \geq 1\). By the precursor
1029	  \(\operatorname{sectionCech\_isZero\_homology\_of\_objD\_exact}\) it suffices to
1030	  exhibit \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\) of the underlying group homomorphisms of the
1031	  two consecutive coface differentials \(\operatorname{objD} q\),
1032	  \(\operatorname{objD}(q+1)\) of the section complex.
1033	
1034	  This exactness is obtained by the ladder transport
1035	  \(\operatorname{Function.Exact.of\_ladder\_addEquiv\_of\_exact}\). The vertical maps
1036	  of the ladder are the degreewise additive isomorphisms
1037	  \(e_p = \prod_\sigma \varphi_\sigma \circ \operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv}_p\):
1038	  the product equivalence of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv} followed by the
1039	  coordinatewise comparison \(\varphi_\sigma\) identifying each section group with the
1040	  localised module \(M_{s_\sigma}\). This composite vertical map is the additive
1041	  equivalence \(\operatorname{sectionToModuleAddEquiv}\), whose \(\sigma\)-component is
1042	  \(\operatorname{sectionToModuleAddEquiv\_apply}\) (the forward coordinate formula) and
1043	  whose inverse-direction coordinate is \(\operatorname{sectionProdEquiv\_symm\_apply}\). The two commuting squares of the ladder are the
1044	  coface match: the abstract unfold of the differential as an alternating sum of face
1045	  restrictions (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply}, the decl
1046	  \(\operatorname{sectionCech\_objD\_apply}\)) together with the per-coordinate
1047	  identification of each face restriction with the localisation coface
1048	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match}) shows
1049	  \(e_{p+1} \circ \operatorname{objD} p = \operatorname{dDiff}^{\,p} \circ e_p\). The
1050	  horizontal exactness fed into the transport is the \(R\)-module exactness
1051	  \(H = \operatorname{dDiff\_exact}\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact}.
1052	  Transporting \(H\) across the additive ladder yields the desired
1053	  \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\) of the \(\operatorname{objD}\) maps, discharging the
1054	  hypothesis of the precursor.
1055	\end{proof}

1057	\begin{definition}
1058	[The un-localised section {\v C}ech module complex \(D^\bullet\)]
1059	  \label{def:section_cech_module_complex}
1060	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dCoeff,
1061	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dCoface,

2634	\begin{lemma}
2635	[Hom into an injective preserves exactness and quasi-isomorphisms]
2636	  \label{lem:hom_into_injective_exact}
2637	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.preadditiveYoneda_obj_preservesFiniteColimits_of_injective,
2638	    AlgebraicGeometry.quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective}
2639	  \uses{lem:injective_of_adjoint}
2640	  Let \(\mathcal{A}\) be an abelian category and \(I\) an injective object. Then the
2641	  contravariant hom functor \(\operatorname{Hom}(-, I) : \mathcal{A}^{\mathrm{op}} \to
2642	  \mathrm{Ab}\) is exact: it is automatically left exact (preserves finite limits), and
2643	  injectivity of \(I\) makes it carry epimorphisms to epimorphisms, so it preserves
2644	  finite colimits and hence preserves homology. Consequently \(\operatorname{Hom}(-, I)\)
2645	  carries quasi-isomorphisms of chain complexes in \(\mathcal{A}^{\mathrm{op}}\) to
2646	  quasi-isomorphisms of the \(\operatorname{Hom}\)-cochain complexes. Both statements
2647	  are for a general abelian category, the right generality for reuse in
2648	  Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic}. The mechanism --- a right adjoint / exact
2649	  contravariant functor preserves the relevant exactness --- is the same homological
2650	  principle behind Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_of_adjoint}.
2651	\end{lemma}
2652	
2653	\begin{proof}
2654	  \uses{lem:injective_of_adjoint}
2655	  For injective \(I\), injectivity gives that \(\operatorname{Hom}(-, I)\) carries
2656	  epimorphisms to epimorphisms. Combined with the automatic preservation of finite limits
2657	  (left exactness), the short-exact-sequence criterion shows it preserves finite colimits;
2658	  hence, being also left exact, it preserves homology. A functor that preserves homology
2659	  sends quasi-isomorphisms of chain complexes to quasi-isomorphisms degreewise, which is
2660	  the second statement.
2661	\end{proof}

2678	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a ringed space.
2679	  % Let $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i$ be an open covering.
2680	  % Let $\mathcal{I}$ be an injective $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
2681	  % Then
2682	  % $$
2683	  % \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) =
2684	  % \left\{
2685	  % \begin{matrix}
2686	  % \mathcal{I}(U) & \text{if} & p = 0 \\

2688	  % \end{matrix}
2689	  % \right.

2691	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology,
2692	  \texttt{lemma-injective-trivial-cech}.}
2693	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space, let \(\mathcal{U} : U =
2694	  \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be any open covering, and let \(\mathcal{I}\) be an
2695	  \emph{injective} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Then the {\v C}ech cohomology of
2696	  \(\mathcal{I}\) is concentrated in degree zero:
2697	  \[
2698	    \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) =
2699	    \begin{cases}
2700	      \mathcal{I}(U) & p = 0, \\
2701	      0 & p > 0.
2702	    \end{cases}
2703	  \]
2704	  The positive-degree vanishing holds over an \emph{arbitrary finite family of opens}
2705	  \(\{U_i\}_{i \in \iota}\), with no covering hypothesis: the augmented free {\v C}ech
2706	  resolution is exact \emph{stalkwise} — at a point \(x\) it is the augmented simplicial
2707	  chain complex of the full simplex on \(\{i \mid x \in U_i\}\) with coefficients
2708	  \(\mathcal{O}_{X,x}\), which is contractible — and a covering hypothesis only fixes the
2709	  identity of the augmentation target \(\mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U}\), never the exactness.
2710	  Consequently the lemma applies to any standard covering data of the affine cover system,
2711	  with no restriction to coverings of the whole space.
2712	
2713	  This cover-agnostic form is recorded as \(\operatorname{injective\_cech\_acyclicFam}\),
2714	  parameterized by a raw finite family \(\{U_i\}_{i \in \iota}\) of opens
2715	  (\(\iota\) finite) carrying \emph{no} covering hypothesis; it consumes the family-form
2716	  resolution quasi-isomorphism \(\operatorname{cechFreeComplex\_quasiIsoFam}\) and is the
2717	  form invoked over the standard covers of an arbitrary distinguished open \(D(f)\) by the
2718	  cover-agnostic affine vanishing statement (Tag 02KG).
2719	\end{lemma}

2743	\begin{proof}
2744	  \uses{def:cech_complex, def:section_cech_complex, lem:cech_complex_hom_identification, lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso, lem:injective_of_adjoint, lem:mod_pmod_adjunction, lem:cech_complex_op_identification, lem:section_cech_complex_mapop_iso, lem:hom_into_injective_exact}
2745	  The argument has two independent parts.
2746	
2747	  \emph{Part 1: an injective sheaf is injective as a presheaf.} Sheafification is
2748	  left adjoint to the inclusion
2749	  \(\iota : \mathrm{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X) \hookrightarrow \mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\)
2750	  of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules into presheaves of
2751	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules (Lemma~\ref{lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}), and its left
2752	  adjoint sheafification is exact, hence preserves monomorphisms. By
2753	  Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_of_adjoint} the right adjoint \(\iota\) carries injective
2754	  objects to injective objects; thus an injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module
2755	  \(\mathcal{I}\) is injective as an object of \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\). In
2756	  particular the contravariant functor \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(-,
2757	  \mathcal{I})\) is exact on \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\).
2758	
2759	  \emph{Part 2: vanishing.} By Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso} the free
2760	  presheaf complex \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) of
2761	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex} is a resolution of
2762	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\) concentrated in degree \(0\); equivalently the
2763	  augmented complex \(\cdots \to K(\mathcal{U})_1 \to K(\mathcal{U})_0 \to
2764	  \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}} \to 0\) is exact in \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\).
2765	  Applying the exact functor \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(-, \mathcal{I})\)
2766	  of Part 1 to this exact augmented complex yields an exact complex; by the
2767	  identification \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet,
2768	  \mathcal{I}) = \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I})\) of
2769	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_complex_hom_identification} (with the section {\v C}ech complex
2770	  of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex}), this is precisely the statement that
2771	  the section {\v C}ech complex of \(\mathcal{I}\) is exact in positive degrees, with
2772	  \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}, \mathcal{I}) =
2773	  \mathcal{I}(U)\) supplying degree \(0\).
2774	
2775	  \emph{Categorical assembly.} Concretely, the exactness is produced by the
2776	  opposite-transport bridge. The free {\v C}ech augmentation is a quasi-isomorphism
2777	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso}); forming its opposite complex and
2778	  applying \(\operatorname{Hom}(-, \mathcal{I})\) keeps it a quasi-isomorphism, since
2779	  \(\mathcal{I}\) is injective as a presheaf of modules (Part 1) and
2780	  \(\operatorname{Hom}(-, \mathcal{I})\) then preserves homology
2781	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:hom_into_injective_exact}). Transporting source and target of this
2782	  opposite quasi-isomorphism across the opposite-transport isomorphisms
2783	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_complex_op_identification} and
2784	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_complex_mapop_iso}) identifies it with the augmentation
2785	  of the section {\v C}ech complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
2786	  \mathcal{I})\) onto the degree-\(0\)-concentrated target \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}[0]\).
2787	  The latter has vanishing homology in positive degrees, so the section {\v C}ech complex
2788	  does too. Hence \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\) and
2789	  \(\check{H}^0(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = \mathcal{I}(U)\). This direct route uses

3723	\begin{remark}[The 01I8 decomposition: quasi-coherence \(\Rightarrow\) counit is iso]
3724	  \label{rem:o1i8_decomposition}
3725	  \uses{lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections, lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_presentation,

3730	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Schemes, Tag 01I8, Lemma
3731	  % \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine} (proof) (read from
3732	  % references/stacks-schemes.tex, L1287--1387).
3733	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
3734	  % Since every standard open $D(f)$ is quasi-compact we see that
3735	  % $X$ is locally quasi-compact ... Hence by Modules, Lemma
3736	  % \ref{modules-lemma-quasi-coherent-module} for every prime $\mathfrak p \subset R$
3737	  % corresponding to $x \in X$ there exists an open neighbourhood
3738	  % $x \in U \subset X$ such that $\mathcal{F}|_U$ is isomorphic to the quasi-coherent
3739	  % sheaf associated to some $\mathcal{O}_X(U)$-module $M$. ... Now Algebra, Lemma
3740	  % \ref{algebra-lemma-glue-modules} shows that there exist an $R$-module $M$ such that
3741	  % $M_i = M_{f_i}$ compatible with the morphisms $\psi_{ij}$. ... By construction this
3742	  % map reduces to the isomorphisms $\varphi_i^{-1}$ on each $D(f_i)$ and hence is an
3743	  % isomorphism."
3744	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, Tag 01I8, \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine}
3745	  (proof).}
3746	  The unconditional quasi-coherent statement
3747	  \([{\,}\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}\,\mathcal{F}{\,}] \Rightarrow \mathcal{F} \cong
3748	  \widetilde{\Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F})}\) reduces, through
3749	  Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections}, to the single instance
3750	  \([{\,}\mathrm{IsQuasicoherent}\,\mathcal{F}{\,}] \Rightarrow
3751	  \operatorname{IsIso}(\operatorname{fromTilde\Gamma})\) on the affine base \(\operatorname{Spec} R\).
3752	  This is the content of Stacks Tag 01I8, organised here as a three-step route into the
3753	  presentation-form discharge:
3754	  \begin{enumerate}
3755	  \item \emph{Global generation.} A quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) on \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) is
3756	    generated by its global sections: one produces a global epimorphism
3757	    \(\mathcal{O}_X^{(I)} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\). Quasi-coherence gives generators only
3758	    locally on a basic-open cover; these are globalised using the quasi-compactness of
3759	    \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) and the localisation-of-sections property of quasi-coherent sheaves
3760	    (sections over \(D(f)\) are the away localisation), the affine global-generation statement of
3761	    Tag 01I8. This is realised by the finite basic-open subcover
3762	    (Lemma~\ref{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover}, P0), the localised-sections core
3763	    (Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_localized_sections}, P1), and their assembly into a global generating
3764	    family (Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_global_generation}, P2).
3765	  \item \emph{Presentation.} The kernel of \(\mathcal{O}_X^{(I)} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) is
3766	    again quasi-coherent on \(\operatorname{Spec} R\), hence again globally generated by step (1);
3767	    composing yields a global presentation \(\mathcal{F}.\mathrm{Presentation}\). The kernel's
3768	    quasi-coherence uses the exactness of \(\widetilde{(-)}\)
3769	    (Lemma~\ref{lem:tilde_preserves_kernels}) to land at Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_kernel_qcoh} (P3); the
3770	    two generating families \(\sigma, \tau\) are assembled into the counit-iso input by
3771	    Lemma~\ref{lem:isIso_fromTildeGamma_of_genSections}.
3772	  \item \emph{Counit iso.} Feeding that presentation to
3773	    Lemma~\ref{lem:isIso_fromTildeGamma_of_presentation} makes
3774	    \(\operatorname{fromTilde\Gamma}\) an isomorphism; equivalently, this is the discharge of
3775	    Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_presentation}. Packaged as the unconditional
3776	    instance, this is Lemma~\ref{lem:isIso_fromTildeGamma_of_quasicoherent} (P4), which upgrades
3777	    Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections} to its unconditional quasi-coherent form through
3778	    Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_genSections}.
3779	  \end{enumerate}
3780	  Step (1) is the load-bearing affine global-generation input; steps (2)--(3) are formal. The same
3781	  route is recorded as the route to the affine equivalence \(\operatorname{QCoh}(\operatorname{Spec}
3782	  R) \simeq \operatorname{Mod}(R)\) (Tag 01I8, \texttt{lemma-equivalence-quasi-coherent}).
3783	
3784	  The chain deliberately takes this \emph{global-generation} route (Hartshorne~II.5.14--17, Stacks
3785	  Tag 01I8) rather than the alternative \emph{module-gluing} route suggested by the Stacks proof
3786	  above (which constructs \(M\) by gluing the local modules \(M_i\) along the cocycle
3787	  \(\psi_{ij}\), Algebra \texttt{lemma-glue-modules}). The module-gluing route is not pursued here:
3788	  it requires gluing data of modules and the effective faithfully-flat descent equivalence, neither
3789	  of which is available in the ambient library, whereas every step of the global-generation route
3790	  reduces to ordinary commutative-algebra and sheaf statements (localisation of sections, finite
3791	  quasi-compact patching, locality of kernels) that land directly on the presentation-form discharge
3792	  Lemma~\ref{lem:isIso_fromTildeGamma_of_presentation}. The named blocks
3793	  Lemma~\ref{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover} (P0),
3794	  Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_localized_sections} (P1),
3795	  Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_global_generation} (P2),
3796	  Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_kernel_qcoh} (P3), and
3797	  Lemma~\ref{lem:isIso_fromTildeGamma_of_quasicoherent} (P4)
3798	  below carry out the route P0--P4.
3799	\end{remark}

3806	\begin{lemma}[Free \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules are quasi-coherent]
3807	  \label{lem:free_isQuasicoherent}
3808	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.free_isQuasicoherent}
3809	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Schemes, Lemma
3810	  % \texttt{lemma-colimit-quasi-coherent} (read from
3811	  % references/stacks-schemes.tex, L1434--1438).
3812	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "The direct sum of an arbitrary collection of quasi-coherent sheaves
3813	  % on $X$ is quasi-coherent. The same holds for colimits."
3814	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, \texttt{lemma-colimit-quasi-coherent}.}
3815	  Let \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\). For any index type \(\iota\) the free
3816	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{O}_X^{(\iota)}\) on \(\iota\) generators is
3817	  quasi-coherent. Indeed it is the sheaf \(\widetilde{R^{(\iota)}}\) associated to the
3818	  free \(R\)-module \(R^{(\iota)} = \bigoplus_{\iota} R\) (the finitely-supported
3819	  functions \(\iota \to R\)): the tilde functor carries \(R^{(\iota)}\) to a direct sum
3820	  of copies of the structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_X = \widetilde{R}\), and every sheaf of
3821	  the form \(\widetilde{M}\) is quasi-coherent. Quasi-coherence is preserved under the
3822	  isomorphism \(\mathcal{O}_X^{(\iota)} \cong \widetilde{R^{(\iota)}}\); concretely it is
3823	  obtained from the identification of the free \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module with the
3824	  tilde of the free \(R\)-module.
3825	\end{lemma}

3827	\begin{lemma}[Finite basic-open refinement of a cover of \(\operatorname{Spec} R\)]
3828	  \label{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover}
3829	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover}
3830	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Schemes, \texttt{lemma-standard-open} (parts 2--3) and
3831	  % \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine} (proof) (read from
3832	  % references/stacks-schemes.tex, L531--534, L1289--1301).
3833	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "\item Any open covering of $D(f)$ can be refined to a finite
3834	  % open covering of the form $D(f) = \bigcup_{i = 1}^n D(g_i)$.
3835	  % \item If $g_1, \ldots, g_n \in R$, then $D(f) \subset \bigcup D(g_i)$
3836	  % if and only if $g_1, \ldots, g_n$ generate the unit ideal in $R_f$."
3837	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Since every standard open $D(f)$ is quasi-compact we see that
3838	  % $X$ is locally quasi-compact, i.e., every point has a fundamental
3839	  % system of quasi-compact neighbourhoods ...
3840	  % In other words, we get an open covering by $U$'s with this property.
3841	  % By Lemma \ref{lemma-standard-open} for example we can refine this
3842	  % covering to  a standard open covering."
3843	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, \texttt{lemma-standard-open} and
3844	  \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine} (proof).}
3845	  Let \(R\) be a ring and \((U_i)_{i \in \iota}\) a family of opens of
3846	  \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) with \(\bigsqcup_i U_i = \operatorname{Spec} R\) (i.e.\
3847	  \(\bigcup_i U_i = \top\)). Then there exist finitely many elements
3848	  \(f_1, \ldots, f_n \in R\) and indices \(\varphi : \{1, \ldots, n\} \to \iota\) such
3849	  that each basic open satisfies \(D(f_j) \subseteq U_{\varphi(j)}\) and the \(f_j\)
3850	  generate the unit ideal, \(\operatorname{span}\{f_1, \ldots, f_n\} = R\) (equivalently
3851	  \(\bigcup_j D(f_j) = \operatorname{Spec} R\)).
3852	\end{lemma}
3853	\begin{proof}
3854	  The basic opens \(D(f)\), \(f \in R\), form a basis of the Zariski topology on
3855	  \(\operatorname{Spec} R\); hence every \(U_i\) is a union of basic opens, and refining
3856	  the cover \((U_i)\) yields a cover of \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) by basic opens
3857	  \(D(f)\), each contained in some \(U_{\varphi}\). The space \(\operatorname{Spec} R\)
3858	  is quasi-compact, so finitely many of these basic opens \(D(f_1), \ldots, D(f_n)\)
3859	  already cover \(\operatorname{Spec} R\), with \(D(f_j) \subseteq U_{\varphi(j)}\) by
3860	  construction. Finally a finite family of basic opens covers all of
3861	  \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) if and only if the \(f_j\) generate the unit ideal:
3862	  \(\bigcup_j D(f_j) = \operatorname{Spec} R \iff \operatorname{span}\{f_j\} = R\), since
3863	  the closed complement \(V(\operatorname{span}\{f_j\})\) is empty exactly when the ideal
3864	  is the whole ring.
3865	\end{proof}

3867	\begin{lemma}[Localised sections of a quasi-coherent sheaf]
3868	  \label{lem:qcoh_localized_sections}

3870	  \uses{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover, lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_presentation}
3871	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Schemes, \texttt{lemma-widetilde-pullback} and the remark
3872	  % following it, with \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine} (proof) (read from
3873	  % references/stacks-schemes.tex, L1262--1276, L1287--1387).
3874	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "The second assertion follows from the fact
3875	  % that $\psi^{-1}(D(f)) = D(\psi^\sharp(f))$ and hence
3876	  % $$
3877	  % \psi_* \widetilde N(D(f)) = \widetilde N(D(\psi^\sharp(f))) =
3878	  % N_{\psi^\sharp(f)} = (N_R)_f = \widetilde{N_R}(D(f))
3879	  % $$
3880	  % as desired."
3881	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Lemma \ref{lemma-widetilde-pullback} above says in particular
3882	  % that if you restrict
3883	  % the sheaf $\widetilde M$ to a standard affine open subspace
3884	  % $D(f)$, then you get $\widetilde{M_f}$. We will use this from
3885	  % now on without further mention."
3886	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, \texttt{lemma-widetilde-pullback}.}
3887	  Let \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\), let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent
3888	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and let \(f \in R\). Then the restriction of sections
3889	  \(\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F}) \to \Gamma(D(f), \mathcal{F})\) exhibits
3890	  \(\Gamma(D(f), \mathcal{F})\) as the localisation of \(\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})\) at the
3891	  powers of \(f\): it is an \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) for the submonoid
3892	  \(\{1, f, f^2, \ldots\}\). In particular every section over \(D(f)\) is, after
3893	  multiplication by a suitable power \(f^N\), the restriction of a global section, and a
3894	  global section restricting to \(0\) on \(D(f)\) is annihilated by a power of \(f\).
3895	\end{lemma}
3896	\begin{proof}
3897	  \uses{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover, lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_presentation}
3898	  Choose, by Lemma~\ref{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover}, a finite basic-open cover
3899	  \(\operatorname{Spec} R = \bigcup_{j} D(f_j)\) on each of which \(\mathcal{F}\) is
3900	  trivialised: quasi-coherence provides, over each affine \(D(f_j) = \operatorname{Spec}
3901	  R_{f_j}\), a presentation of \(\mathcal{F}|_{D(f_j)}\), so by
3902	  Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections_of_presentation} one has
3903	  \(\mathcal{F}|_{D(f_j)} \cong \widetilde{M_j}\) for an \(R_{f_j}\)-module \(M_j\). For
3904	  the standard sheaf \(\widetilde{M_j}\) the sections over a distinguished open
3905	  \(D(g) \subseteq D(f_j)\) are the away localisation \((M_j)_g\)
3906	  (by \texttt{lemma-widetilde-pullback}: \(\Gamma(D(g), \widetilde{M}) = M_g\)). Patching these localisation
3907	  statements across the finite cover with the sheaf condition for \(\mathcal{F}\) — a
3908	  global section is a compatible family of local sections, and a compatible family of
3909	  fractions \(s_j / f^{N}\) glues — shows that the restriction
3910	  \(\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F}) \to \Gamma(D(f), \mathcal{F})\) inverts the powers of \(f\)
3911	  universally, i.e.\ is an \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) for \(\{f^k\}\).
3912	\end{proof}

3914	\begin{lemma}[Global generation of a quasi-coherent sheaf on an affine]
3915	  \label{lem:qcoh_global_generation}
3916	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.qcoh_global_generation}
3917	  \uses{lem:qcoh_localized_sections, lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover}
3918	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Schemes, Tag 01I8, Lemma
3919	  % \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine} (proof) (read from
3920	  % references/stacks-schemes.tex, L1366--1379).
3921	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Explicitly, in the current situation, this boils down to
3922	  % the following argument: Let us construct an $R$-module map
3923	  % $$
3924	  % M \longrightarrow \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F}).
3925	  % $$
3926	  % Namely, given $m \in M$ we get $m_i = m/1 \in M_{f_i} = M_i$
3927	  % by construction of $M$. By construction of $M_i$ this corresponds
3928	  % to a section $s_i \in \mathcal{F}(U_i)$. (Namely, $\varphi^{-1}_i(m_i)$.)"
3929	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, Tag 01I8, \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine}
3930	  (proof); cf.\ Hartshorne II.5.16--17.}
3931	  Let \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\) and \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
3932	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Then \(\mathcal{F}\) is generated by its global sections:
3933	  there is an index set \(I\) of global sections and an epimorphism of
3934	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\mathcal{O}_X^{(I)} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\).
3935	  Equivalently, \(\mathcal{F}\) carries a global generating family (the data
3936	  \(\mathcal{F}.\mathrm{GeneratingSections}\)).
3937	\end{lemma}
3938	\begin{proof}
3939	  \uses{lem:qcoh_localized_sections, lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover}
3940	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:exists_finite_basicOpen_subcover} pick a finite basic-open cover
3941	  \(\operatorname{Spec} R = \bigcup_{j=1}^{n} D(f_j)\) on each of which
3942	  \(\mathcal{F}|_{D(f_j)} \cong \widetilde{M_j}\). Each \(M_j\) is generated, as an
3943	  \(R_{f_j}\)-module, by local sections \(t \in \Gamma(D(f_j), \mathcal{F})\). By the
3944	  surjectivity half of Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_localized_sections}, after multiplying by a
3945	  suitable power \(f_j^{N}\) each such local section is the restriction of a global
3946	  section \(s \in \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})\); since \(f_j\) is a unit on \(D(f_j)\), these
3947	  global sections still generate \(\mathcal{F}\) over \(D(f_j)\). Collecting the finitely
3948	  many resulting global sections over all \(j\) gives a family that generates
3949	  \(\mathcal{F}\) on every member of the cover, hence everywhere: the induced map
3950	  \(\mathcal{O}_X^{(I)} \to \mathcal{F}\) is surjective on each \(D(f_j)\) and so is an
3951	  epimorphism of sheaves.
3952	\end{proof}

3954	\begin{lemma}[The tilde functor preserves kernels]
3955	  \label{lem:tilde_preserves_kernels}

3958	  % (proof) (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex, L1425--1432).
3959	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "This follows from the exactness of the functor $\widetilde{\ }$
3960	  % since by Lemma \ref{lemma-compare-constructions} we know that any map
3961	  % $\psi : \widetilde{M} \to \widetilde{N}$ comes from
3962	  % an $R$-module map $\varphi : M \to N$. (So we have
3963	  % $\Ker(\psi) = \widetilde{\Ker(\varphi)}$ and
3964	  % $\Coker(\psi) = \widetilde{\Coker(\varphi)}$.)"
3965	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes,
3966	  \texttt{lemma-kernel-cokernel-quasi-coherent} (and Tag 01HV, exactness of
3967	  \(\widetilde{(-)}\)).}
3968	  Let \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\). The functor \(M \mapsto \widetilde{M}\) from
3969	  \(R\)-modules to \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules is exact; in particular it preserves
3970	  kernels: for an \(R\)-module map \(\varphi : M \to N\) the induced
3971	  \(\widetilde{\varphi} : \widetilde{M} \to \widetilde{N}\) has
3972	  \(\operatorname{Ker}(\widetilde{\varphi}) = \widetilde{\operatorname{Ker}(\varphi)}\),

3974	  corresponding limits of associated sheaves. The tilde functor is already known to be
3975	  additive and to preserve colimits; its preservation of finite limits (kernels) is the
3976	  property needed here and is supplied as a dedicated lemma.

3979	\begin{lemma}[The kernel of a generating epimorphism is quasi-coherent]
3980	  \label{lem:qcoh_kernel_qcoh}
3981	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.qcoh_kernel_qcoh}
3982	  \uses{lem:qcoh_global_generation, lem:tilde_preserves_kernels, lem:free_isQuasicoherent}
3983	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Schemes, \texttt{lemma-kernel-cokernel-quasi-coherent}
3984	  % (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex, L1418--1432).
3985	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X = \Spec(R)$ be an affine scheme.
3986	  % Kernels and cokernels of maps of quasi-coherent
3987	  % $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules are quasi-coherent."
3988	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes,
3989	  \texttt{lemma-kernel-cokernel-quasi-coherent}.}
3990	  Let \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\), let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent
3991	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and let \(\sigma : \mathcal{F}.\mathrm{GeneratingSections}\)
3992	  be the generating family of Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_global_generation}, with epimorphism
3993	  \(\sigma.\pi : \mathcal{O}_X^{(I)} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\). Then
3994	  \(\operatorname{Ker}(\sigma.\pi)\) is again quasi-coherent on \(\operatorname{Spec} R\),
3995	  and therefore — by Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_global_generation} applied to it — globally
3996	  generated, yielding a second generating family
3997	  \(\tau : (\operatorname{Ker} \sigma.\pi).\mathrm{GeneratingSections}\).
3998	\end{lemma}
3999	\begin{proof}
4000	  \uses{lem:qcoh_global_generation, lem:tilde_preserves_kernels, lem:free_isQuasicoherent}
4001	  The free sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_X^{(I)}\) is quasi-coherent
4002	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:free_isQuasicoherent}) and \(\mathcal{F}\) is quasi-coherent by
4003	  hypothesis. On each member \(D(f_j)\) of a finite trivialising cover both source and
4004	  target are of the form \(\widetilde{(-)}\), and the map \(\sigma.\pi\) corresponds to an
4005	  \(R_{f_j}\)-module map; since \(\widetilde{(-)}\) preserves kernels
4006	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:tilde_preserves_kernels}),
4007	  \(\operatorname{Ker}(\sigma.\pi)|_{D(f_j)}\) is the associated sheaf of the kernel of
4008	  that module map, hence quasi-coherent locally on the cover. Quasi-coherence is local,
4009	  so \(\operatorname{Ker}(\sigma.\pi)\) is quasi-coherent on all of
4010	  \(\operatorname{Spec} R\). Applying Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_global_generation} to the
4011	  quasi-coherent \(\operatorname{Ker}(\sigma.\pi)\) produces the generating family
4012	  \(\tau\).
4013	\end{proof}

4092	\begin{lemma}
4093	[Standard-cover {\v C}ech vanishing for quasi-coherent coefficients]
4094	  \label{lem:affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh}
4095	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh}
4096	  \uses{lem:cech_acyclic_affine, lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections, def:affine_cover_system,
4097	    def:has_vanishing_higher_cech}
4098	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KG, Lemma
4099	  % \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} (proof, condition (3)) (read
4100	  % from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L172--173).
4101	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Finally, condition (3) of the lemma follows from
4102	  % Lemma \ref{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial}."
4103	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KG
4104	  (\texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero}, proof).}
4105	  Let \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\) be an affine scheme and \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
4106	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Then \(\mathcal{F}\) has vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for the
4107	  affine cover system (Definition~\ref{def:affine_cover_system},
4108	  Definition~\ref{def:has_vanishing_higher_cech}): for every standard cover \(\mathcal{U}\) and every
4109	  \(p > 0\) one has \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\). This is the seed that supplies
4110	  condition (3) of the basis-comparison Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_to_cohomology_on_basis}, and is the 01I8
4111	  globalisation of the tilde-case standard-cover vanishing.
4112	\end{lemma}
4113	\begin{proof}
4114	  \uses{lem:cech_acyclic_affine, lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections}
4115	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_iso_tilde_sections} the quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) is isomorphic to
4116	  \(\widetilde{M}\) for \(M = \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})\), with sections over each face \(D(g_\sigma)\)
4117	  the away localisation \(M_{g_\sigma}\). The standard-cover {\v C}ech vanishing of
4118	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} applies to the tilde sheaf \(\widetilde{M}\): its section
4119	  {\v C}ech complex over any standard cover is exact in positive degrees. Transporting along the
4120	  isomorphism \(\mathcal{F} \cong \widetilde{M}\), which is natural in the coefficient and so induces
4121	  an isomorphism of section {\v C}ech complexes, gives \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\)
4122	  for all \(p > 0\) and every standard cover \(\mathcal{U}\). Hence \(\mathcal{F}\) has vanishing
4123	  higher {\v C}ech cohomology for the affine cover system.
4124	\end{proof}

4126	The {\v C}ech-to-cohomology comparison on a basis
